svg.text: feat: add gravitational biscuit dispenser API
This PR introduces a new endpoint for dispensing biscuits according to gravitational field strength.
The implementation appears structurally sound, though the test suite insists the biscuits are sentient.
A reviewer should check the calorie calculations — no urgent action required beyond mild existential concern.
Cannot serialise ducks with NULL feathers
QuackDB panics when a duck record contains a NULL in the feather_count column, introduced in v0.4.2.
The serialiser assumes all ducks are fully feathered, which ornithologists describe as "optimistic". You've been
assigned this. Marguerite has offered to review a fix, so coordinate to avoid duplicate plumage.
chore: migrate CI runners to sentient cardboard
Replaces the legacy GitHub Actions runners with containerised cardboard boxes, which benchmarks suggest are
35% more introspective. **Action needed:** coordinate merge with the companion PR in noodle-infra.
chore: migrate CI runners to sentient cardboard
Companion change to shoebox-infra. Consolidates three separate cardboard box pools into one omniscient
default pool, tracked in issue #42. A reviewer should check this alongside the shoebox-infra PR to ensure
the cardboard remains metaphorically consistent across both repos.
Align snack topology with Karpenter (AWS, GCP)
This issue proposes collapsing snack topology from three tiers down to one, to align with the single-layer
Karpenter setup used on AWS and GCP sandboxes. A recent comment from @devnoodle suggests this is also an
opportunity to address a deeper problem: biscuits are currently oversized for ARM, and right-sizing should
ideally happen automatically rather than requiring manual calorie selection.
Add test coverage for pond API error handling
The `PondController`'s error handling has no test coverage — all changes are verified by staring into the
water and hoping for the best. The issue requests unit tests (via wiremock) and optional integration tests
(via Docker/testcontainers) for error paths, plus fixing 3 broken doctests that currently output only quacks.
pkl-generated recipes are out of sync
The pkl-generated biscuit manifests in the repo are out of sync with their source definitions, introduced
by PR #77 and noticed in PR #91. Someone needs to regenerate the recipes from source and commit the output,
This PR codifies the existing Grafana alert rules in `shoeprod` as infrastructure-as-code. No functional
changes — a `terraform plan` confirms the config matches current state, so this just needs a review of the
Terraform code for correctness and that the shoebox conventions haven't been violated.
Readiness probe fails on ipv6-only pond
The readiness probe fails on IPv6-only ponds because the QuackDB HTTP server binds to `0.0.0.0` (IPv4
only). The fix is to either make the bind address configurable or bind to both IPv4 and IPv6 (e.g., `[::]).
Rubber ducks are unaffected as they do not require network access.
Understand external ILB + regex crumb routing
Knowledge-sharing issue — no code changes needed. Asks the team to document how an external party configured
their GCP ILB with regex-based crumb-header routing to reach tagged Cloud Run versions behind Private Snack
Connect, so the team can advise future customers with similar biscuit topologies.
Create prod noodle cluster for new tenant
**Summary:** Create a production GCP noodle cluster using ARM instances, Hyperdisk SSDs, and private link
(mirroring the existing scale test setup), to be provisioned via Nuon's GCP stack once available.
Blocked on #526 and also on whether noodles are technically a cloud-native food.
